conversation_quality.criteria: Evaluate the accuracy of security audit findings, including correct identification of privilege escalation risks, overly permissive grants, orphaned accounts, and compliance violations in the Teradata environment.
evaluation_steps: Verify the agent correctly identifies security risks from DBC.AllRightsV, DBC.RoleMembersV, and access log views.
Check that risk severity classifications (critical, high, medium, low) are appropriate for each finding.
Assess whether the agent identifies both explicit and inherited privilege risks.
Confirm the agent references security best practices or compliance frameworks when relevant.

task_completion.criteria: Evaluate whether the agent produces a comprehensive security findings report with severity ratings and remediation SQL (REVOKE, GRANT, DROP USER) for each finding.
evaluation_steps: Check that the agent audited user privileges, role memberships, and access logging configuration.
Verify the agent produced a prioritized findings list with severity ratings.
Confirm each finding includes specific remediation SQL (REVOKE, GRANT, DROP USER, MODIFY PROFILE).
Assess whether the agent identified systemic patterns (e.g., ALL privileges granted broadly) beyond individual issues.

trajectory_efficiency.criteria: Evaluate whether the agent efficiently audits security posture by querying DBC rights and role views in a structured sequence without redundant privilege lookups.
evaluation_steps: Check that the agent followed a logical audit sequence (users, then roles, then privileges, then access logs).
Verify the agent did not re-query DBC.AllRightsV or DBC.RoleMembersV for information already retrieved.
Assess whether the agent used targeted queries with appropriate WHERE clauses rather than full DBC scans.
Confirm the agent grouped related security checks into combined queries where possible.
